duration: 1st sighting was 10 minutes and 2nd sighting was 30 minutes
specific_location: First seen at Swede Lake, which is on the east end of Fossil Creek Reservoir. It then flew over to Duck Lake, which is the second place that we saw it.
description: When it was sitting: The bird was slightly larger than the Franklin's Gulls that was around. It had black primaries, a dark gray mantle and scapulars (the same color throughout the back, not dark and light gray), it had gray on the hind neck and sides of the breast area. quite a bit of black on the head (not as much as 1st summer, but more than 2nd winter). Had a larger bill than Franklin's Gulls and it was drooped at the tip, it was a black bill, and there was no black on the tail while it was sitting. When it was flying: It had very little black on the tail and extensive black on the upper and lower primaries.Behaviors: It was around Franklin's Gulls, but it didn't habg out with them. Swimming around in the water.Call: didn't hear itPlumage: In between 1st Summer and 2nd Winter
